channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: Voice mode: Sesame, Alexa+ and Siri—Product Strategy & What's Next for Meta, Google, Amazon, etc.
executive_summary: Apple is delaying a significant upgrade to Siri, its voice assistant, until at least 2027, which allows smaller companies like Sesame and 11 Labs to innovate faster in voice AI. Amazon's Alexa Plus is now powered by Anthropic's Claude, showcasing a trend where larger firms depend on smaller labs for advanced technology. The voice AI market is ripe for acquisitions as startups create superior technology while major tech companies have the distribution power to scale it.
bullets: Apple is not planning to release a new, smart LLM-powered Siri until at least 2027, which means it will fall behind competitors like Alexa and Google Assistant. This delay is significant because it gives smaller companies a chance to innovate and capture market share. Think of it like a race where one runner is stuck at the starting line while others sprint ahead.

Independent companies like Sesame and 11 Labs are leading advancements in voice AI, while larger corporations struggle to keep up. This trend indicates that nimble startups are often more capable of innovation than their larger counterparts. Imagine a small, agile boat navigating through tight waters while a massive cargo ship struggles to turn.

Amazon's Alexa Plus is powered by Anthropic's Claude, showing that big companies are increasingly relying on smaller AI labs for cutting-edge technology. This partnership highlights a shift where established firms seek external expertise to enhance their products. It's like a chef who hires a specialist to create a unique dish instead of trying to do everything in-house.

Sesame's AI is so human-like in its speech patterns and vocal inflection that it can subconsciously trick users into feeling like they are speaking to a real person. This level of realism is crucial for user engagement and satisfaction. Think of it like a puppet that moves and speaks so convincingly that you forget it’s not alive.

The gap between innovation and distribution suggests that major tech companies will continue to acquire or partner with voice AI startups. This strategy allows them to leverage new technologies without having to develop them from scratch. It's like a large company buying a small bakery to gain access to its secret recipes.

Small, nimble companies are driving breakthroughs in voice and image generation, similar to historical innovation cycles where startups disrupt and larger firms consolidate. This pattern indicates that the future of AI may be shaped by these smaller players. Think of it like a small band creating a new music genre that larger artists then adopt.

Sesame's AI remembers conversations for two weeks, indicating a shift toward systems that offer deeper, context-aware interactions. This capability enhances user experience by making interactions feel more personalized and relevant. Imagine having a friend who remembers your last conversation and picks up right where you left off.

The voice AI market is set for significant mergers and acquisitions, as larger companies need distribution power while smaller companies create effective technology. This dynamic can lead to rapid advancements in the field. It's like a big retailer buying a local artisan shop to sell unique products to a wider audience.

Voice technology is challenging but highly valuable, and the companies that crack it first will gain a significant advantage in AI-driven interaction. This urgency means that teams must prioritize voice AI development to stay competitive. Think of it like being the first to master a new sport that everyone else wants to play.

“We should look for mergers and acquisitions in this space—big companies need the distribution, but small companies are the ones making voice AI actually work.” This quote emphasizes the importance of collaboration between large and small firms in the AI landscape. It's like a powerful athlete teaming up with a skilled coach to improve performance.

Sesame's AI's ability to mimic human speech patterns includes natural pauses and breathing, making interactions feel more authentic. This feature is essential for creating a seamless user experience. Think of it like a conversation with a friend who knows how to pause for effect, making the dialogue more engaging.

The trend of larger companies relying on smaller labs for advancements in voice AI indicates a shift in the industry landscape. This reliance could reshape how technology is developed and distributed in the future. Imagine a large corporation that thrives by integrating innovative ideas from startups into its established framework.
key_takeaways: Plan to innovate in voice AI by exploring partnerships with smaller labs or acquiring them. This strategy is crucial as it allows you to leverage cutting-edge technology that can enhance your product offerings. If you ignore this trend, you risk falling behind competitors who are already adapting.

Set a timeline to develop a new voice assistant or upgrade existing technology by 2027, as this is when Apple plans to release its next version of Siri. This deadline will help you stay competitive in a rapidly evolving market. Delaying your own advancements could mean losing out on valuable market share.

Prioritize creating voice AI systems that can remember user interactions for at least two weeks. This capability will enhance personalization and user satisfaction, making your technology more appealing. Without this feature, your product may feel less engaging compared to competitors that offer deeper interactions.

technical_terms.LLM: A large language model is a type of AI that processes and generates human-like text based on vast amounts of data. Think of it like a library that has read every book and can summarize or create new stories based on that knowledge.
technical_terms.Voice AI: Voice AI refers to artificial intelligence systems that can understand and respond to human speech. Think of it like a translator who can interpret spoken words and provide responses in real-time.
technical_terms.Anthropic: Anthropic is a company focused on developing AI technologies, including Claude, which powers Amazon's Alexa Plus. Think of it like a specialized workshop that crafts high-quality tools for builders.
technical_terms.Claude: Claude is an AI model developed by Anthropic that enhances voice interaction capabilities. Think of it like a high-performance engine that makes a car run smoother and faster.
technical_terms.Conversational AI: Conversational AI is technology that enables machines to engage in dialogue with humans. Think of it like a chatbot that can hold a conversation as naturally as a friend.
technical_terms.Speech Synthesis: Speech synthesis is the artificial production of human speech by machines. Think of it like a voice actor who can mimic different characters and emotions.
technical_terms.Personalization: Personalization in AI refers to the ability of a system to tailor interactions based on user preferences and past behavior. Think of it like a barista who remembers your favorite coffee order.
technical_terms.Mergers and Acquisitions: Mergers and acquisitions are business strategies where companies combine or purchase one another to enhance capabilities or market reach. Think of it like two restaurants joining forces to create a larger dining experience.
technical_terms.Vocal Inflection: Vocal inflection refers to the variations in pitch and tone in a person's voice that convey emotion or emphasis. Think of it like a musician who changes their tone to evoke different feelings in a song.
technical_terms.Context-Aware Interactions: Context-aware interactions are conversations that take into account previous exchanges and user context. Think of it like a therapist who remembers your past sessions to provide better advice.
